$schema_note: Machine-readable training/eval set for BI Python reasoning. Fictional retail scenarios only. Score an agent on reasoning match + correct ending artifact. Mirrors references/agent-training-set.md.
seed_note: Initial seed. Several items' ends_on targets name artifacts that are planned / not yet implemented in this seed (dataframe-review-checklist, merge-fanout-checklist, validation-reconciliation-checklist, date readiness verdict, performance diagnostic verdict). The shipped checklists are checklists/aggregation-grain-checklist.md and checklists/cleaning-review-checklist.md. Until the others ship, score on reasoning quality and accept the verdict produced inside the live knowledge file as the artifact. This is a seed/eval set, not proof the skill is complete.

items.question: store_id loaded as integers and a join to stores dropped rows. Why?
items.expected_reasoning: Numeric keys lose leading zeros and/or mismatch string keys, so matches silently fail; keys must be string-typed.

items.question: Revenue is 8% high after joining orders to returns. What happened?
items.expected_reasoning: One-to-many fan-out duplicated order lines; summing revenue double-counts. Aggregate returns to order-line grain, then merge many-to-one.

items.question: Should I fillna(0) on discount_amt before averaging?
items.expected_reasoning: No by default; null != zero and it changes the mean. Decide skip vs treat-as-zero explicitly and record; imputation deferred to metric owner.

items.question: Number of orders per store - which operation?
items.expected_reasoning: nunique(order_id); frame is order-line grain, so size overcounts.

items.question: Can I sum each region's average basket value to get the company average?
items.expected_reasoning: No; averages are non-additive. Recompute from base totals (weighted average).

items.question: channel shows 6 values but the domain has 3. Fix?
items.expected_reasoning: Trim + lowercase, confirm collapse to exactly {store,web,app}; flag survivors rather than auto-mapping.

items.question: Dates parse differently across files, smearing monthly revenue. Fix?
items.expected_reasoning: Pin format per source, coerce failures to NaT, record parse rate; treat format change as schema drift.

items.question: A join to customers linked many unrelated guest orders. Why?
items.expected_reasoning: Empty-string customer_id keys matched each other; convert empty string to null before joining.

items.question: Can I mark the frame reconciled because the numbers look right?
items.expected_reasoning: No; reconciliation needs control-total evidence (expected/actual/delta) with declared tolerance.

items.question: The notebook is slow with a row loop computing net line value. Fix?
items.expected_reasoning: Vectorize the column expression; diagnose the real constraint first; stop at fast-enough.

items.question: Should I compute YTD revenue in pandas for the dashboard?
items.expected_reasoning: No; period metrics are DAX measures. Python prepares clean datetime and period keys only.

items.question: Several columns are object dtype in the ready frame. Acceptable?
items.expected_reasoning: No; resolve each to a real dtype or justify it. object means uninferred and costs memory.

items.question: Can I drop_duplicates() to clean up the frame?
items.expected_reasoning: Only after declaring the uniqueness key and measuring duplicates; distinguish exact dupes from key collisions with differing attributes.

items.question: Grouped revenue total is lower than ungrouped. Why?
items.expected_reasoning: Null group keys were dropped from groups; make null explicit or include it; reconcile before/after totals.

items.question: Python validated everything; can I skip the readiness gate?
items.expected_reasoning: No; Python validates and produces a reconciliation record, readiness owns gating. Hand off the record.
